Notice And Motion For Order Appointing Referee
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING REFEREE.

Appearances: Marc Bender of the Law Office of Marc D. Bender (650.638.2347) for defendant and opposing party; Stanley Riddell of Bornstein Law (415.409.7611) for plaintiffs and moving parties.

Counsel report that they have agreed on Benjamin Graves to be appointed as referee. Plaintiff will submit a proposed order and interlocutory judgment in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Mr. Riddell will inform department 206 to vacate the trial date of October 13, 2025. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice And Motion For Order Appointing Referee
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Continued To Oct-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stanley Riddell for plaintiff Clayton L Yee.
Marc D. Bender for defendant Gilbert Tang (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Plaintiff's Motion for Order Appointing Referee.

During roll call for the Law & Motion Calendar, Mr. Bender indicated that he contested the Court's tentative decision to appoint Amy Harrington as referee, arguing that under CCP 873.040 consent is required from all parties as to the appointment. He claimed to have stated in an opposition filed 9/3/2025 that defendant did not consent to the appointment of Ms. Harrington.

When the matter was called, Mr. Bender was no longer present on Zoom. The Court suggested continuing the matter to allow time for the parties to nominate proposed referees. Following discussion, the Court ruled as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING REFEREE is continued to October 8, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 501. Plaintiff shall give notice of the continued hearing date.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice And Motion For Order Appointing Referee

Stanley Riddell for plaintiffs Clayton L. Yee and Grace Tang.

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING REFEREE is continued to October 1, 2025 for parties to meet and confer on possibility of sale without appointment of referee. If parties do not reach a final agreement reduced to writing, the Court will grant the motion appointing a referee.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D501)
